---
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    title-block-style: none
---

```{=html}
<!-- ── Site Header ───────────────────────────── -->
<header class="site-header">
  <div class="container">
    <span class="site-name">Shilpa Sundar &nbsp;·&nbsp; Portfolio</span>
  </div>
</header>

<!-- ── Hero + Timeline wrapper (shared continuous watermark) ── -->
<div class="hero-timeline-wrapper">

<!-- ── Hero / About ──────────────────────────── -->
<section id="about">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="hero">
      <img class="headshot" src="assets/headshot.jpg" alt="Shilpa Sundar">
      <div class="hero-text">
        <h1>Shilpa Sundar, PharmD, MPS</h1>
        <p class="hero-subtitle">PhD Researcher &nbsp;·&nbsp; Biomedical AI &amp; Knowledge Graphs &nbsp;·&nbsp; UNC Chapel Hill</p>
        <div class="bio">
          <p>Shilpa is a PhD researcher at UNC Chapel Hill, working in the Translational and Integrative Sciences Lab (TISLab) on biomedical knowledge graphs and graph deep learning. Her current focus is rare disease drug repurposing and drug-drug interaction prediction.</p>
          <p>Before the PhD, she spent over five years as a healthcare consultant and commercial data analyst at Clarivate Analytics and Trinity Life Sciences, advising pharma and MedTech clients across EU, US, and JAPAC markets on pricing, market access, and forecasting. That clinical and commercial background still shapes how she approaches modeling — she cares about whether methods hold up against messy biomedical data, not just on a benchmark.</p>
          <p>Other notes: 2023 Penn Healthcare Case Competition winner (Wharton), 2024 Nucleate RTP Finalist, and a participant in the 2025 AWS Open Science Hackathon on Knowledge Graphs.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="social-links">
          <a href="https://github.com/Shilpasundar14" class="social-btn" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-github"></i> GitHub</a>
          <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/drshilpasundar/" class="social-btn" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-linkedin"></i> LinkedIn</a>
          <a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0003-4097-3395" class="social-btn" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-orcid"></i> ORCID</a>
          <a href="mailto:shilpasundar1214@gmail.com" class="social-btn"><i class="fas fa-envelope"></i> Email</a>
          <a href="assets/resume.pdf" class="social-btn cv" download="Shilpa Sundar Resume.pdf"><i class="fas fa-download"></i> Download CV</a>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- ── Career Timeline ───────────────────────── -->
<section id="timeline" class="timeline-section">
  <div class="container">
    <h2 class="section-title">Career</h2>
    <div class="timeline">
      <div class="timeline-item">
        <span class="timeline-year">2013</span>
        <span class="timeline-event">PharmD — Manipal University, India</span>
      </div>
      <div class="timeline-item">
        <span class="timeline-year">2019</span>
        <span class="timeline-event">Associate Consultant &rarr; Research Associate — Clarivate Analytics</span>
      </div>
      <div class="timeline-item">
        <span class="timeline-year">2022</span>
        <span class="timeline-event">Associate Consultant — Trinity Life Sciences</span>
      </div>
      <div class="timeline-item">
        <span class="timeline-year">2023</span>
        <span class="timeline-event">MPS in Biomedical &amp; Health Informatics — UNC Chapel Hill (GPA 4.0)</span>
      </div>
      <div class="timeline-item">
        <span class="timeline-year">2024</span>
        <span class="timeline-event">PhD Candidate — TISLab, UNC Chapel Hill</span>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

</div><!-- /.hero-timeline-wrapper -->

<!-- ── Projects ──────────────────────────────── -->
<section id="projects">
  <div class="container">
    <h2 class="section-title">Projects</h2>
    <div class="projects-grid">

      <!-- 1. RotatE -->
      <div class="project-card">
        <div class="project-title">RotatE Knowledge Graph Embeddings</div>
        <p class="project-desc">Took the RotatE knowledge graph embedding model and applied TracInCP influence scoring to figure out which training subgraphs the model is actually relying on. The point is interpretability — when a KG embedding makes a prediction, you want to know which training facts pushed it that way, not just trust the score.</p>
        <div class="tags">
          <span class="tag">Knowledge Graphs</span>
          <span class="tag">Graph Embeddings</span>
          <span class="tag">RotatE</span>
          <span class="tag">TracInCP</span>
          <span class="tag">PyTorch</span>
        </div>
        <figure class="project-figure-wrap">
          <img class="project-figure" src="https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/b9/Biomedical_Knowledge_Graph_in_Wikidata.svg" alt="Biomedical knowledge graph schematic">
          <figcaption class="img-credit">Image: <a href="https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Biomedical_Knowledge_Graph_in_Wikidata.svg" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Wikimedia Commons</a> — Andrew Su, CC BY 4.0</figcaption>
        </figure>
        <a href="https://github.com/Shilpasundar14/RotatEKnowledgeGraphEmbedding/tree/rotate_CCDDgraph_tracin" class="btn-github" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub</a>
      </div>

      <!-- 2. Biomedical KG Scoring -->
      <div class="project-card">
        <div class="project-title">Biomedical KG Relevance Scoring</div>
        <p class="project-desc">A scoring rubric for the 91 primary knowledge sources that feed into ROBOKOP-KG and RTX-KG2, built as part of the Every Cure Matrix project. Each source gets evaluated on four criteria and tagged HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW relevance — so anyone downstream can actually justify the sources they're pulling into a drug repurposing pipeline.</p>
        <div class="tags">
          <span class="tag">Knowledge Graphs</span>
          <span class="tag">Drug Repurposing</span>
          <span class="tag">Biomedical AI</span>
          <span class="tag">FAIR</span>
        </div>
        <img class="project-figure" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Shilpasundar14/biomedical-kg-relevance-scoring/main/figures/scoring_framework_infographic.jpg" alt="Scoring Framework Infographic">
        <a href="https://github.com/Shilpasundar14/biomedical-kg-relevance-scoring" class="btn-github" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub</a>
      </div>

      <!-- 3. Monarch KG -->
      <div class="project-card">
        <div class="project-title">Monarch Knowledge Graph Cluster Analysis</div>
        <p class="project-desc">Clustered the Monarch Initiative knowledge graph — genes, diseases, and the relationships between them — using K-means and hierarchical methods to see how gene-disease associations group together. t-SNE, UMAP, and heatmaps for the visualizations.</p>
        <div class="tags">
          <span class="tag">Knowledge Graphs</span>
          <span class="tag">Clustering</span>
          <span class="tag">t-SNE</span>
          <span class="tag">UMAP</span>
          <span class="tag">R</span>
        </div>
        <img class="project-figure" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Shilpasundar14/Monarch-Knowledge-Graph-Analysis-Project/main/figures/labelled_tsne_plot.png" alt="t-SNE cluster plot">
        <a href="https://github.com/Shilpasundar14/Monarch-Knowledge-Graph-Analysis-Project" class="btn-github" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub</a>
      </div>

      <!-- 4. NLP / BERT -->
      <div class="project-card">
        <div class="project-title">NLP Sentiment Analysis — BERT &amp; Transformers</div>
        <p class="project-desc">Fine-tuned BERT, DistilBERT, RoBERTa, and BERTweet on the Kaggle NLP Sentiment Analysis XM competition (4-class tweet sentiment). Added one improvement at a time — LR scheduling, early stopping, cross-validation — to see what each was actually worth. The underlying question: does pretraining on 850M+ tweets (BERTweet) beat a general-purpose transformer here?</p>
        <div class="tags">
          <span class="tag">NLP</span>
          <span class="tag">Transformers</span>
          <span class="tag">BERT</span>
          <span class="tag">BERTweet</span>
          <span class="tag">Fine-tuning</span>
          <span class="tag">Python</span>
        </div>
        <img class="project-figure" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Shilpasundar14/nlp-sentiment-analysis-bert/main/figures/model_accuracy_comparison.png" alt="Model accuracy comparison">
        <a href="https://github.com/Shilpasundar14/nlp-sentiment-analysis-bert" class="btn-github" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub</a>
      </div>

      <!-- 5. Rainfall Prediction -->
      <div class="project-card">
        <div class="project-title">Binary Rainfall Prediction — 14-Model ML Pipeline</div>
        <p class="project-desc">14-model comparison for Kaggle Playground Series S5E3 (binary rainfall prediction). Worked up from single classifiers to voting ensembles, stratified K-fold ensembles, and finally two-layer stacking — each step compared against the previous one. The two-layer stack with an LGBM meta-learner trained on out-of-fold predictions ended up giving the best AUC-ROC.</p>
        <div class="tags">
          <span class="tag">Machine Learning</span>
          <span class="tag">Ensemble Methods</span>
          <span class="tag">Stacking</span>
          <span class="tag">LightGBM</span>
          <span class="tag">XGBoost</span>
          <span class="tag">Python</span>
        </div>
        <img class="project-figure" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Shilpasundar14/binary-rainfall-prediction/main/plots/Combined%2014%20models%20plot.png" alt="14 model comparison plot">
        <a href="https://github.com/Shilpasundar14/binary-rainfall-prediction" class="btn-github" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub</a>
      </div>

      <!-- 6. DDI Benchmark -->
      <div class="project-card">
        <div class="project-title">Comparative DDI Prediction Benchmark</div>
        <p class="project-desc">A side-by-side benchmark of GNN-based DDI prediction models — vanilla GNNs, SumGNN, KGNN, and EmerGNN — across molecular structures, pharmacological networks, and biomedical knowledge graphs. The aim is to figure out which approach actually generalizes, not just which scores highest on one dataset. Manuscript in prep.</p>
        <div class="tags">
          <span class="tag">Graph Neural Networks</span>
          <span class="tag">Drug-Drug Interactions</span>
          <span class="tag">Deep Learning</span>
          <span class="tag">Biomedical AI</span>
        </div>
        <figure class="project-figure-wrap">
          <img class="project-figure" src="https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/1/1e/GNN_building_blocks.png" alt="Graph neural network building blocks">
          <figcaption class="img-credit">Image: <a href="https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:GNN_building_blocks.png" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Wikimedia Commons</a> — NickDiCicco, CC BY-SA 4.0</figcaption>
        </figure>
        <a href="https://github.com/Shilpasundar14/Comparative-DDI-Prediction-Benchmark-Study" class="btn-github" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub</a>
      </div>

      <!-- 7. Legal Interoperability -->
      <div class="project-card">
        <div class="project-title">Legal Interoperability in Biomedical KGs</div>
        <p class="project-desc">A licensing audit of 39 biomedical data sources, treating legal constraints as actual FAIR infrastructure rather than something you deal with at the end. Each source ends up in one of five operational reuse categories based on license type, commercial permissions, redistribution rights, and ShareAlike obligations. Manuscript in prep.</p>
        <div class="tags">
          <span class="tag">FAIR Data</span>
          <span class="tag">Biomedical KGs</span>
          <span class="tag">Legal Interoperability</span>
          <span class="tag">Data Governance</span>
        </div>
        <figure class="project-figure-wrap">
          <img class="project-figure" src="https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/8/8b/FAIR_data_principles.svg" alt="FAIR data principles diagram">
          <figcaption class="img-credit">Image: <a href="https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:FAIR_data_principles.svg" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Wikimedia Commons</a> — SangyaPundir, CC BY-SA 4.0</figcaption>
        </figure>
        <a href="https://github.com/Shilpasundar14/Licensing-and-Terms-of-Use-Assessment" class="btn-github" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub</a>
      </div>

    </div>
  </div><!-- /.container -->
</section>

<!-- ── Footer ─────────────────────────────────── -->
<footer class="footer">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="footer-links">
      <a href="mailto:shilpasundar1214@gmail.com" class="footer-link"><i class="fas fa-envelope"></i> shilpasundar1214@gmail.com</a>
      <a href="https://github.com/Shilpasundar14" class="footer-link" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-github"></i> GitHub</a>
      <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/drshilpasundar/" class="footer-link" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-linkedin"></i> LinkedIn</a>
      <a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0003-4097-3395" class="footer-link" target="_blank"><i class="fab fa-orcid"></i> ORCID</a>
    </div>
    <p class="copyright">&copy; 2026 Shilpa Sundar</p>
  </div>
</footer>
```
